visual_features.overall_style: Clean, minimalist layout with strong emphasis on whitespace and a single bold artistic logo. The overall feel is contemporary and restrained, allowing the art-related imagery and brand mark to stand out. It combines a neutral, almost default system-style UI (links, text) with an expressive hand-drawn logotype.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, weight, and spacing. The large, black, hand-drawn 'TOSKA' logo at the top is the primary focal point. Below it, underlined purple text links act as secondary focal elements. On the right, the address block is smaller and lighter, serving as tertiary information. The 'BOOK ONLINE' section title, set in spaced-out uppercase letters, becomes the next anchor, followed by the large two-column service card where the bright yellow image block on the left pulls attention before the 'Let's Meet' text content on the right.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is generous, especially vertically. There is a large gap between the logo/links zone and the 'BOOK ONLINE' heading, and again between the heading and the first service card. The layout breathes with wide margins on the left and right, and elements are not crowded. This creates a calm, editorial feel and lets each section read clearly.
unique_elements: Hand-drawn, heavy-black logotype for 'TOSKA' that contrasts with the otherwise standard sans-serif body text.
Highly simplified top zone with only text links (no traditional navbar container) centered under the logo.
Service card with a strong half-and-half composition: full-height yellow image on the left and white text panel on the right separated by a subtle border.
Section title style 'BOOK ONLINE' using widely letter-spaced uppercase text as a minimalist typographic motif.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width content area on a 12-column grid, with the service card split roughly 6/6 between image and text.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Single-column page with centered header and text, followed by left-aligned content sections. The service offering uses a full-width card with a two-column split inside.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top section: logo centered, text links beneath, address block right-aligned in the same horizontal band. Middle section: large whitespace, then the 'BOOK ONLINE' section label aligned left in the main content column. Below that: a full-width service block composed of two columns—left image, right text. Additional services would presumably stack vertically in the same pattern.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Global content is center-aligned at the top for brand and navigation-like links, while primary content (section heading and service description) is left-aligned. Address block is right-aligned, creating an asymmetrical but balanced header band. Text inside the service card is left-aligned, maintaining readability.
responsive_hints: Centered header and wide margins suggest a single, constrained content column that can easily adapt to smaller screens.
Two-column service card (image + text) likely collapses into vertically stacked blocks on mobile (image first, text below).
Typography and spacing appear large and comfortable, which typically translates well to touch devices.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. The page begins with a vertically centered brand block: large logo at top, two centered inline text links below, and a right-aligned address in the same horizontal tier. After a substantial vertical gap, a left-aligned 'BOOK ONLINE' section title appears. Beneath that, the first visible service is presented as a wide rectangular card. Inside this card, content is arranged in two equal-width columns: on the left, a large yellow background with angled art-related cards; on the right, a text block with the service title 'Let's Meet', a short description paragraph, duration, and price. Services would likely continue in a vertical stack of similar full-width cards.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Top header band (logo + links + address) occupies the upper portion with generous top margin around the logo. A large block of negative space follows before 'BOOK ONLINE', emphasizing section separation. The service card itself takes up a substantial horizontal width of the content column, with the left image column slightly more visually dominant due to color saturation, even though the width is roughly equal. Vertical padding inside the card is moderate, keeping content comfortably spaced without feeling dense. Overall, whitespace margins around sections account for a significant portion of the viewport height, contributing to a light, uncluttered feel.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious editorial layout with ample breathing room. Elements are well separated by large vertical margins, and there are relatively few UI controls or dense data groupings in view. The design prioritizes clarity and focus on a small number of key elements (logo, section title, current service card) over showing many options at once.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye first lands on the bold black 'TOSKA' logo at the top center, then moves down to the purple underlined links just beneath it. From there, it is drawn rightwards to the address block, completing a gentle horizontal sweep. The large white space then pulls the gaze downward to the 'BOOK ONLINE' headline on the left, establishing the start of the main content. From this heading, the bright yellow image in the first service card becomes the next strong attractor on the left, then the eye shifts to the right to read the 'Let's Meet' text block. This creates a subtle reversed-S or staggered Z-pattern: logo → links → address → down to section title → left image → right text.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Dedicated button elements are not clearly visible in the captured area. Any booking CTA buttons elsewhere in this design are likely to follow the same minimalist style: flat rectangular shapes with slight corner rounding (2–4px radius), solid fill in the accent color (purple) or neutral black, and simple centered text using a sans-serif font. Borders would be minimal or absent, relying on fill color and text contrast. Hover states would probably adjust background color or add an underline to maintain consistency with the link style.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure would be semantic

ui_patterns.visual_description: The service block functions as a card: a wide rectangle with a subtle light grey border (#E0E0E0) and no visible shadow, creating a flat, box-based separation from the white page background. Corners appear sharp or minimally rounded (0–2px radius). Inside, the card is divided into two equal-width columns; the left column is filled entirely with an image that has a solid yellow background, while the right column is a white panel containing text. Internal padding in the right column is moderate (likely 24–32px on all sides), giving the title, description, and meta information room to breathe. Typography hierarchy inside the card is clear: larger, bold title at top; normal-weight paragraph; smaller meta info (duration, pricing) spaced below.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form controls are not visible in this screenshot, but given the minimalist aesthetic, inputs are likely to be simple rectangular text fields with light grey borders, white backgrounds, and no heavy shadows. Labels would likely appear above inputs in a small, regular-weight sans-serif, keeping vertical flow clean. Validation would probably be indicated with subtle color changes (border and helper text) rather than strong icons.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected layout pattern is stacked labels with full-width inputs inside form sections, e.g., <label> above <input>, each wrapped in a <div class="form-group">. Multi-step booking might group related inputs in logical sections, possibly using <fieldset> and <legend> for accessibility.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Navigation in the captured view is represented by two simple text links under the logo. They are center-aligned, underlined, and styled in a bright purple accent color, resembling default browser link styling. There is no visible nav bar background, border, or container; links float in whitespace, enhancing the minimalist feel. Active/visited states are likely expressed via link color or underline style. The right-aligned address acts as a static informational block rather than navigation.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
HTML is likely a simple inline list of elements, potentially inside a
or
, for example:
How to use Toska...Benefits of Toska...
. Centering is probably handled by text-align on the parent container. No dropdowns or complex menus are implied in this view.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible service block suggests a list-of-cards pattern for multiple services: each item as a large, full-width card stacked vertically with significant spacing between them. Item density is low, prioritizing readability and imagery. There are no visible separators besides vertical spacing and the card borders themselves. Within each item, the image occupies the left side and acts as a visual thumbnail, while text on the right provides hierarchical information (title, description, meta).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Such a list would typically be structured as a <div class="service-list"> containing repeated <article> or <div class="service-card"> elements. Internally, each card follows a flexbox layout for media + content. Alternatively, a semantic <ul> with each <li> containing a card would also fit, especially if multiple services are rendered dynamically.

heading.visual_style: The logotype is a custom, hand-drawn, heavy display style with soft, irregular shapes and very thick strokes. Section headings like 'BOOK ONLINE' appear to use a simple sans-serif with expanded letter-spacing and all caps, creating a clean, understated heading style.
heading.recommended: For section headings (not the logo), a font like 'Space Grotesk' or 'DM Sans' would match the clean, geometric/modern sans look while still feeling contemporary and artistic.

body.visual_style: Body text appears to be a neutral sans-serif with regular stroke contrast and moderate x-height, similar to system fonts like Arial or Helvetica. It’s unobtrusive and functional.
body.recommended: IBM Plex Sans or Work Sans would be well-suited for body copy, providing clarity and a refined, contemporary character.

font_sizes.h1: 32px–40px (for section titles; the logo is larger as an image graphic)

spacing_system.component_padding: Cards and content blocks appear to use around 24–32px of internal padding on larger screens, aligning with 3x–4x the base 8px unit.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major vertical gaps between sections (e.g., between logo area and 'BOOK ONLINE', and between 'BOOK ONLINE' and the first card) appear to be in the 64–96px range, roughly 8x–12x the base unit.

interaction_hints: Text links are underlined and colored in purple, suggesting standard browser-like hover behavior (color shift and possibly underline persistence on hover).
Buttons (if present elsewhere) would likely use simple hover effects: slightly darker background, subtle color shift, or underline added to text, maintaining the flat aesthetic.
Card areas might respond to hover with a very light background tint change or border-color change to indicate clickability without adding heavy shadows.

Any animations should be minimal and smooth (150–250ms transitions) to keep the calm, editorial feel—e.g., fade-in/fade-out for hover states and gentle transform: translateY(-2px) for card hover, if used.
